extensionDescription.message: Provider add-on for TbSync that synchronizes contacts, calendars, and tasks with an Exchange ActiveSync server.

setup.intro.message: Choose the type of Exchange ActiveSync account you want to add.

setup.accountType.personalMs.hint.message: outlook.com, hotmail.com, live.com and other consumer Microsoft accounts.

setup.accountType.auto.hint.message: Look up the EAS server URL from your email address. Works for most Exchange and EAS-compatible providers.

setup.accountType.custom.hint.message: Any EAS-compatible server. Username and password authentication.

setup.auto.error.title.message: We couldn’t find an EAS server

setup.auto.error.auth.message: The username or password was rejected. Check spelling, or fall back to “Custom EAS server”.

setup.auto.error.network.message: Could not reach the discovery endpoints. Check your internet connection.

setup.auto.error.noServer.message: Discovery completed but no EAS server was advertised. The server may not support auto-detect.

setup.auto.error.fallback.message: If your provider requires manual configuration, choose “Custom EAS server”.

setup.oauth.error.emailRequired.message: Please enter your Microsoft account email.

setup.error.missingToken.message: Missing setup token. Open this window through TbSync.

config.password.message: Password (leave blank to keep the current one)

config.readOnlyBanner.message: To prevent synchronization errors, settings cannot be edited while the account is enabled.

config.protocol.provision.hint.message: Some servers (e.g. Kerio) require provisioning to be sent before any other command. Most servers - including Microsoft Exchange and Microsoft 365 - request it automatically when needed; you do not need to enable this manually for them.

config.contacts.displayOverride.message: Override Display Name with "First Name" + "Second Name"

config.calendar.syncRecurrence.message: Sync recurring events / tasks and exceptions

config.calendar.syncRecurrence.hint.message: Experimental - off by default. When enabled, recurrence rules and per-instance exceptions round-trip with the server.

warning.reauthFailed.message: Re-authentication did not complete. Click "Sign in again" to retry.

options.intro.message: These settings affect every EAS account in this profile. The defaults work for almost everyone, only change a value if you have a specific reason to. Leave a field empty to fall back to the bundled default.

options.timeout.hint.message: Maximum time to wait for a response from the EAS server before giving up. Increase this if you sync over slow or unreliable links and see frequent timeouts. Default: 90000 ms (90 seconds).

options.useragent.hint.message: HTTP User-Agent string sent with every EAS request. A few servers refuse the default and require a specific value to allow the connection. Default: Thunderbird ActiveSync.

options.devicetype.hint.message: EAS DeviceType value sent in the request URL. A few servers reject unknown device types. Default: TbSync.

options.maxitems.hint.message: Maximum number of items the server may return per sync request. Larger values sync faster on healthy connections, smaller values are more reliable on flaky ones, use less memory and give a better feedback in the UI. Default: 25.

options.mstodo.hint.message: Microsoft To Do publishes tasks with a date-only due date and no start date, so reminders render at midnight. When enabled, an incoming task with a reminder is rewritten so its start and due date both equal the reminder time, and the reminder fires exactly when the task is due. Only enable this when encountering issues and trying an alternative to the default behavior.

options.clientid.hint.message: Microsoft Entra Client ID used when signing in to Microsoft accounts. Leave empty to use the default client ID registered by John Bieling (author of the EAS provider).
